description:
Advances: specled.realized_by

Deliverable:
Extend `SpecLedEx.Realization.Orchestrator.attestations/2` to walk each subject's `verification` blocks of `kind: tagged_tests`. For every requirement listed under `covers:` whose `realized_by` bindings produced attestations on the prior pass, expand the requirement's test files (looked up via `index["test_tags"][requirement_id]`) into the subject's attestation map, carrying the same MFA list as the production-code attestation. Requirements whose bindings drifted or were dangling do not produce test-file attestations.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: tagged_tests for specled.realized_by.attestation_tagged_tests_expansion in test/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator_test.exs. Flip its verification entry to execute: true.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios specled.realized_by.scenario.attestation_tagged_tests_expand, specled.realized_by.scenario.attestation_tagged_tests_drifted_requirement_no_expand.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/binding.ex
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md
- .spec/specs/binding.spec.md

Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex
- test/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator_test.exs
- .spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md (only to flip execute: false → execute: true on specled.realized_by.attestation_tagged_tests_expansion)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT consume the attestation map in branch_check yet — that's the next ticket.
- Do NOT broaden expansion to non-tagged_tests verification kinds (source_file etc.). Only tagged_tests targets expand into test-file paths.
- Do NOT change the attestation-map shape. Test-file entries use the same `{:attested_clean, [mfa]}` value shape; the path key is the test file's normalized relative path.
- Do NOT change `run/2`'s return shape.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification entry for attestation_tagged_tests_expansion flipped to execute: true
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Tests added (all tagged @tag spec: "specled.realized_by.attestation_tagged_tests_expansion"):
- "requirement-level binding that attests clean expands tagged_tests covers into test-file attestations"
- "tag entries with string-keyed file shape are also recognized"
- "requirement whose binding drifted produces no test-file attestation"
- "non-tagged_tests verification kinds do not trigger expansion"
- "tagged_tests covering a requirement with no realized_by binding does not expand"
- "tagged_tests covering a requirement whose test_tag entry is missing yields no test attestation"

Verification stubs flipped: specled.realized_by.attestation_tagged_tests_expansion (execute: false → true)

Summary:
Extended `Orchestrator.build_attestations/4` to walk each subject's
`kind: tagged_tests` verification blocks. For each requirement under `covers:`
whose `realized_by` bindings produced clean attestations on this run, the
orchestrator looks up the requirement's test files via
`index["test_tags"][requirement_id]` and adds a
`{:attested_clean, [mfas]}` entry to the subject's attestation map for each
test file, carrying the same MFA list as the production-code attestation.
The drift-set check applied to production bindings naturally filters out
drifted/dangling requirements: they never make it into the per-requirement
clean-MFA bucket, so the expansion produces nothing for them.

Implementation notes:
- Per-requirement clean-MFA tracking happens in the same reduce that builds
  production attestations; no second pass over bindings.
- Subject-layer bindings (requirement_id == nil) contribute to production
  attestation but never to tagged_tests expansion — the expansion is
  requirement-scoped by design.
- Implementation-tier subjects always carry requirement_id == nil after
  aggregation, so they cannot drive tagged_tests expansion either. This
  matches the spec's wording ("requirement listed under `covers:` whose
  `realized_by` bindings produced attestations") since tagged_tests covers
  are requirement ids, not subject ids.
- The expansion gates strictly on `kind == "tagged_tests"`; other verification
  kinds (source_file, etc.) do not expand.
- Test entry shape supports both `%{file: "..."}` (TagScanner output) and
  `%{"file" => "..."}` (hand-built test indexes).

Merge-gate checks (on this stage worktree):
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
- mix test: 794 tests, 0 failures
- mix format --check-formatted (on touched files): clean
- mix deps.unlock --check-unused: clean
- mix spec.check: no findings against specled.realized_by; pre-existing
  branch_guard findings remain (out of scope; specled_-3rs.4 will address)

Pre-existing format issues on the epic branch (decision_parser/cross_field.ex,
spec_dedup_realized_by_test.exs, runs_test.exs) are unrelated to this ticket
and unchanged.

- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  - No changes to `branch_check.ex` or `branch_check.spec.md` (next ticket's territory).
  - Expansion is strictly gated on `kind == "tagged_tests"` (verified by the "non-tagged_tests verification kinds do not trigger expansion" test).
  - Attestation map shape preserved: test-file entries reuse `{:attested_clean, [mfa]}` with the normalized test-file path as key.
  - `run/2` return shape unchanged; the new behavior rides on `run_with_attestations/2` / `attestations/2`.
  - No deps added (mix.exs / mix.lock untouched).
- Project verification: PARTIAL
  - `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: clean
  - `mix test`: 794 tests, 0 failures
  - `mix deps.unlock --check-unused`: clean
  - `mix format --check-formatted`: FAIL, but only on files NOT touched by this commit (`decision_parser/cross_field.ex`, `schema/realized_by.ex`, `spec_dedup_realized_by_test.exs`, `coverage/store_test.exs`). Pre-existing on the base ref. Out of scope.
  - `mix spec.check`: 5 errors / 5 warnings, all under `specled.branch_guard` (file_touch_*). Pre-existing and explicitly the territory of the follow-on ticket specled_-3rs.4 ("Branch_check yields to attestation"). No findings under `specled.realized_by`.

Provenance trace:
- `accumulate_tier/7` (lines 510-548) builds binding entries with `requirement_id: nil` for subject-layer and `requirement_id: rid` for requirement-layer; `all_subject_requirement_mfa_triples/1` correctly threads those through. Implementation-tier returns `nil` (aggregated earlier) — consistent with the implementer's note and the spec wording (tagged_tests `covers` lists requirement ids, not subject ids; impl-tier aggregation makes the requirement provenance moot for tagged_tests expansion).
- Tag entry shape (`%{file: ...}` atom-keyed) is what `TagScanner` emits and `Index.normalize_file_entry/2` preserves; `tag_entry_file/1` also handles the string-keyed shape for hand-built indexes — the "string-keyed file shape" test exercises that branch.
- `index["test_tags"][requirement_id]` is read exactly as the spec describes (orchestrator.ex:295).
- Drift-set filtering uses the same `branch_guard_realization_drift` / `branch_guard_dangling_binding` codes the production-attestation path filters on; the "requirement whose binding drifted" test seeds a wrong hash, observes the drift finding, and confirms BOTH production AND test-file entries are absent — matching `scenario.attestation_tagged_tests_drifted_requirement_no_expand`.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- `.spec/state.json` is a regeneration artifact; if the auditor wants it strictly excluded, the implementer would need to either drop it from the commit or have the ticket pre-declare it. Consistent with prior epic commits in this branch line.
- Pre-existing failures in `mix format --check-formatted` and `mix spec.check` are confined to files / subjects this ticket is forbidden from touching. They are the work of specled_-3rs.4 and other tickets. The implementer's framing here is accurate.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: agent-a2667b3d4fddfeab9 (parent b920bd00-78bf-43a9-b891-877e668cc527)
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b920bd00-78bf-43a9-b891-877e668cc527/subagents/agent-a2667b3d4fddfeab9.jsonl

Reasoning: The only `must` requirement this ticket advances — `specled.realized_by.attestation_tagged_tests_expansion` — is genuinely covered by six tests in `test/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator_test.exs` (S7 describe block), and all six would fail if the corresponding behavior were broken:

- `scenario.attestation_tagged_tests_expand` is exercised by the "requirement-level binding that attests clean expands..." test (asserts `inner["test/coverage_test.exs"] == {:attested_clean, [mfa]}` exactly).
- `scenario.attestation_tagged_tests_drifted_requirement_no_expand` is exercised by the "requirement whose binding drifted..." test, which pre-seeds a wrong hash via `HashStore.write/2`, asserts the drift finding is present, and refutes both the test-file AND production-code entries — the test would pass for adjacent reasons only if `drift_dangling_pairs/1` weren't actually consulted, which I verified is the same gate as the prior orchestrator_publishes_attestations path (orchestrator.ex:225, `drift_set = drift_dangling_pairs(findings)`).
- The kind gate at `tagged_tests_verifications/1` (orchestrator.ex around line ~316) is independently tested by the "non-tagged_tests verification kinds do not trigger expansion" test, which constructs a `kind: source_file` verification with a populated `test_tags` entry and refutes the test-file key — this would fail if the gate were removed or weakened.
- The `index["test_tags"][requirement_id]` provenance is called from exactly one site (orchestrator.ex inside `expand_tagged_tests/4`) and that path is the only producer of test-file attestation keys; the "missing test_tag entry" test exercises the empty-lookup fall-through.

Mechanical scope recount against the diff: 4 files touched (`.spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md`, `.spec/state.json`, `lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex`, `test/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator_test.exs`) — matches the verifier. The `.spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md` edit is the single allowed line flip (line 577 `execute: false` → `execute: true`), no other lines changed. `.spec/state.json` is a regeneration artifact (only finding count + corresponding finding rows removed; verification execute toggle). Out-of-scope files (`binding.ex`, `branch_check.ex`, `branch_guard.spec.md`, `binding.spec.md`) are all untouched.

Intent-level scope: `run/2` signature at orchestrator.ex:98 is unchanged (only `run_with_attestations/2` and `build_attestations/4` were modified); the attestation map shape `{:attested_clean, [mfa]}` is preserved (same `put_attestation/4` helper consumed by both phases); branch_check is not consumed (no edits to `branch_check.ex`); expansion is strictly requirement-scoped on `kind == "tagged_tests"` (verified by code at `tagged_tests_verifications/1` and the dedicated test).

Project verification: `mix test` → 794 tests, 0 failures; `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` → clean; the targeted `mix test --only spec:specled.realized_by.attestation_tagged_tests_expansion` → 6 tests, 0 failures (excludes 31 unrelated). `mix spec.check` findings are confined to `specled.branch_guard.file_touch_*` (the territory of follow-on ticket specled_-3rs.4); zero findings against `specled.realized_by`.

One non-blocking observation: the implementation interprets "requirement's `realized_by` bindings" as the requirement's *own* declared bindings (subject-inherited bindings on bare requirements do not drive expansion — see `accumulate_tier/7` at orchestrator.ex:510, where `req_entries` only enumerate `Map.get(rb, tier_key, [])` from the requirement's own `rb`). This matches both named scenarios literally (each carries the binding on the requirement) and the implementer's spelled-out interpretation; it is not a spec violation given the scenario wording, but a future "subject-inherited binding drives tagged_tests expansion" scenario would not yet pass. Calling it out for downstream tickets, not bouncing.
APPROVED. Merged into feature/file-touch-yields-to-realization. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: Tagged-tests attestation expansion
